# Python Delivery Checklist

Before returning Python code, verify:

## Correctness

- [ ] Imports are complete
- [ ] Names are consistent
- [ ] Code is syntactically valid
- [ ] Main logic matches the request
- [ ] Edge cases were considered

## Quality

- [ ] Clear naming
- [ ] Type hints included where useful
- [ ] Functions are reasonably small
- [ ] Error handling is appropriate
- [ ] No unnecessary complexity

## Maintainability

- [ ] Repeated logic extracted if needed
- [ ] Comments are useful, not noisy
- [ ] Docstrings exist for key functions or classes
- [ ] Config is not hardcoded unnecessarily

## Testing

- [ ] Core logic is testable
- [ ] Suggested tests included when valuable
- [ ] Failure scenarios considered

## Performance

- [ ] No obvious wasteful operations
- [ ] Suitable data structures chosen
- [ ] Optimization claims are justified

## Final Output

- [ ] Easy to copy and run
- [ ] Explanation is concise
- [ ] Assumptions are stated
